title: eriogonum strictum subsp. proliferum
intro: Eriogonum strictum subsp. proliferum, also known as desert buckwheat or Nevada buckwheat, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buckwheat family. It is native to the southwestern United States and has been used by various Native American tribes for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant is known to have health benefits such as wound healing, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory issues. Desert buckwheat can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
